mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth, boosting consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising retail sales indicates synchronized supply-demand expansion, validating corporate profit margins and triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative SPX returns in over 60% of triggered periods, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling 24-month regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active rate normalization, rising short-term rates increase borrowing costs for highly leveraged firms, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of tightening episodes, or the negative correlation between rate hikes and equity returns vanishes (correlation > -0.05) over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect sustained consumer confidence and credit availability, leading to immediate positive revisions in economic growth forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% over a 3-year walk-forward test, or housing starts decline while SPX 5-day returns remain positive in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in industrial production exceeding 1.5 percent signals supply chain bottlenecks or capacity constraints, which compresses corporate margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in over 55% of triggered supply contraction events, or the signal's predictive power (Rank IC) drops below 0.02 across any rolling 36-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid employment growth paired with accelerating real personal spending validates broad-based economic expansion, reinforcing earnings upgrade cycles and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint employment-spending signal yields negative SPX returns in more than 58% of instances, or the interaction effect becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.15) in a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary environment where CPI growth falls below 1.5 percent while housing starts remain elevated, real purchasing power expands without triggering Fed tightening, supporting equity valuations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates negative 5-day returns in over 60% of disinflationary housing expansion periods, or the combined signal's directional accuracy drops below 48% over a 2-year out-of-sample test.
